title: Tropical Storm Arthur Made Landfall in Texas. Hurricane Season Starts Early.
script: First storm of the season — landed already? Tropical Storm Arthur made landfall on the Texas coast Tuesday, becoming the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season — and it wasted no time. Arthur formed in the Gulf, strengthened fast, and hit the Bolivar Peninsula hard. At least 2 people are dead. More than 180 flooding reports across Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, and Alabama since Sunday. Over 17 million people are under flood watches from eastern Texas to Georgia. The National Hurricane Center warned of life-threatening flash floods through Friday. Storm surge reached 2 to 4 feet near Morgan City, Louisiana. Meteorologists say this is an unusually early and active start to hurricane season. So: how many named storms are we looking at before this season is over?
